canonical_question: What are the best practices for secrets management?
aliases: how to store API keys securely

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: HashiCorp Vault 1.20 GA (June 2025); AWS Secrets Manager cross-account sharing (2024); SOPS donated to CNCF Sandbox (2023)

constraints: NEVER hardcode secrets in source code, configuration files, or container images -- CWE-798
NEVER commit secrets to version control -- even in private repos, git history retains them permanently
Secrets MUST be encrypted at rest and in transit -- use TLS for all secrets manager communication
Rotate secrets automatically on a schedule appropriate to sensitivity
Apply least-privilege access to every secret -- no blanket read-all permissions
NEVER log secret values -- mask or redact in all application and infrastructure logs

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to store user passwords (hashing, not secrets management)
Need encryption key management (KMS) rather than secret storage
Need OAuth/OIDC token management for end users

inputs_needed.question: What infrastructure or cloud provider are you using?
What type of secrets do you need to manage?
